record.field: Handles building permits, construction approvals, and structural inspections for all construction activities within the municipality.
Manages road maintenance, infrastructure repairs, street lighting, and public utilities across the municipality.
Oversees waste management, environmental complaints, pest control, food safety inspections, and public health services.
Responsible for urban development plans, zoning, signage permits, and maintaining the aesthetic standards of the city.
Manages public facilities, parks, community centers, event spaces, and recreational services for residents.
Issues and renews trade licenses, commercial permits, and business registrations for all commercial activities.

1. Title deed or property ownership certificate 2. Architectural drawings (signed by licensed engineer) 3. Structural engineering report 4. NOC from Civil Defense 5. Site plan with plot boundaries 6. Emirates ID copy of the applicant
Apply for a building permit to construct, renovate, or extend any structure within Sharjah municipality boundaries. All construction activities require prior approval.
1. Photos of the affected area 2. Exact location description or map pin
Report road damage, potholes, broken sidewalks, faulty street lights, or any infrastructure issues that need municipal attention.
1. Photos of the issue 2. Location description
Report illegal dumping, missed waste collection, pest infestations, noise complaints, or any environmental and public health concerns in your area.

1. Trade name reservation certificate 2. Tenancy contract (Ejari) for business premises 3. Passport copy of the owner/partners 4. Emirates ID copy 5. NOC from relevant authority (if applicable) 6. Initial approval from Department of Economic Development
Apply for a new trade license or renew an existing one for commercial, professional, or industrial activities within the municipality.
1. Booking request form 2. Emirates ID copy 3. Event details (type, expected attendees, date/time)
Reserve community halls, parks, sports facilities, or public event spaces managed by the municipality for events, gatherings, or activities.
1. Sign design and dimensions 2. Location photo showing proposed placement 3. Trade license copy 4. NOC from building owner
Apply for permission to install, modify, or replace commercial signage, billboards, or advertising displays within the municipality.
